The probe is tied to alleged hacker Srikrishna Ramesh, known as “Sriki,” accused of siphoning off Bitcoin and other digital assets through cyber intrusions since 2017.

title: CBI Arrests DGCA Deputy Director General in Rs 2.5 Lakh Bribery Case
description: The CBI arrested a Deputy Director General of DGCA’s Airworthiness Directorate and a private company representative in a Rs 2.5 lakh bribery case. Searches in Delhi recovered Rs 37 lakh cash, gold, silver coins, and digital devices. A separate case saw a Superintendent of Post arrested for accepting a Rs 10,000 bribe.

title: Karnataka: Miscreants Vandalise Mahatma Gandhi's Statue in Haveri; Police Probe Initiated

title: UP Police Arrest Two People Involved in Noida Workers' Protest

The state government later announced a 21% minimum wage hike for Noida and Ghaziabad workers, effective April 1, 2026, following the unrest.

title: The Right Approach to Punctuality
Poison in the grain
description: Arsenic from contaminated groundwater is entering the food chain via rice across the Gangetic plains—and climate change is making it worse.

title: Dwarka forest and the misunderstood politics of deemed forests and invasive species
description: The Supreme Court’s dismissal of a challenge to the clearance of over thousands of trees near Delhi’s airport raises hard questions about what counts as a forest when the law privileges planning documents over ecological reality.

title: Geeta Parag: When they did not spare Mirabai, then who am I to complain?
description: The Dalit woman singer from the Malwa region talks about drawing strength from history’s treatment of women saints, arguing that discrimination based on gender and caste must be met with the resolve to keep moving forward.

title: What Cuba’s filmmakers built that Trump cannot sanction away
description: For six decades, Cuban directors made films that outlasted every embargo. Tomás Gutiérrez Alea showed why art and politics cannot be separated.

description: Venkatram Reddy said any reference to the prolonged struggle and sacrifices of students and youth as a political delay amounted to disrespect to the families of martyrs.

description: In addition, special meetings with parents of students will be conducted in government institutions during the week.

description: Other people have their midlife crises in their forties. But not me.

title: Puppy Rescued After Suspected Abuse
description: A 60‑day‑old Labrador puppy, suspected to have been brutally assaulted by a gang, has been rescued by animal activist Shaik Sana, who named the puppy Yodha.

description: Naked man spotted roaming in Old City lanes, probe on

title: Maharashtra To Fast-Track Land Allocation For Data Centres
description: The government has asked the City and Industrial Development Corporation (CIDCO) to develop a land bank to position Maharashtra as a major data infrastructure hub.

title: Health Camps Every Quarter In Jails
description: Minister Damodar Rajanarsimha said on Saturday that the health department would enter into a memorandum of understanding with the prisons to facilitate health check‑ups every three months in every jail across the state.

title: Devi Kar | Retro Attractions: Going Back To The Past Can Often Be A Style Statement!
description: Retro food items, retro music and retro gadgets and gizmos not only feed nostalgia but may enhance our lifestyles

title: Four-Year Scholarships Offered To Rankers By Vignan Varsity
description: An extensive scholarship programme offering financial support to meritorious students for up to four years has been announced by Vignan University during the release of the VSAT–2026 Phase–1 results.

title: Naidu Vows To Clear 100% Waste From Urban Centres By June
description: Naidu said he wants to ensure processing of 100 per cent garbage collected from the present 57 per cent.

title: Haj Committee Gears Up For Pilgrims From May 4
description: Telangana Haj Committee is gearing up for arrangements for pilgrims embarking from RGIA from May 4. A total of 9,660 pilgrims — including 6,660 from Telangana and 2,283 from Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka — will fly from Hyderabad to Jeddah in Saudi Arabia.

description: His warning is less metaphor, more siren: Ignore this blend, and the arithmetic of representation may hand the steering wheel largely to northern states, leaving the south politically sidelined

title: Why Is Revanth Silent On Tejasvi's Slander On Telangana?: Harish
description: Speaking with reporters in Jagtial, Harish Rao said Surya insulted the self-respect of four crore people of Telangana.

title: HMWS&SB To Launch Special Drive Against Water Wastage; Citizen Volunteers To Report Misuse Via ‘Paani’ App
description: The board is supplying 570 million gallons per day (MGD) of drinking water, drawn from projects on the Krishna, Godavari and Manjira and the Osmansagar and Himayatsagar reservoirs.

title: Dr D. Nageshwar Reddy Inducted Into AIMBE College Of Fellows, Among World’s Top 2% Innovators
description: Election to the AIMBE College of Fellows is reserved for the top two percent of medical and biological engineers globally, recognising individuals who have made outstanding contributions to research, innovation, and the translation of science into clinical practice.

title: PM, Amit Shah Lied to Nation on Women’s Bill, Flags ‘Assam, J&K model’ in TN: Rahul
description: The BJP-led government transformed the electoral map of Assam and Jammu and Kashmir and they intend doing it in the rest of the country as well, he alleged.

title: Non‑Alcoholic Liver Disease On The Rise
description: Non-alcoholic liver diseases on the rise, warn doctors

title: Book Review | What Ails Private Hospitals: A Full Diagnosis
description: In setting about the unenviable task of explaining the wide tangle of acronyms, medical procedures and legislations undergirding the system, Ghosh’s book underlines the gulf in information that shapes the relationship between hospitals and their patients in India today

title: Zero Tolerance On Irregularities In Paddy Procurement, Says Kodanda Reddy
description: Kodanda Reddy said farmers need not visit rice mills after selling their produce at procurement centres, adding that officials were responsible for ensuring Minimum Support Price (MSP) to farmers.

title: Kondapalli Toy Festival Begins With Grandeur, Showcasing Timeless Craft
description: The festival also saw participation from several key officials, including former ZP chairperson Gadde Anuradha, state language and culture department director Regulla Mallikarjuna Rao, district tourism officer A. Shilpa, regional director Y.V. Prasanna, and DEO L. Chandrakala: Reports

title: Cathay Pacific Abhijit Abhyankar on Why Hyderabad is Key Global Travel Hub
description: As India’s long-haul travel demand evolves beyond volume to value, Cathay Pacific is recalibrating its network, product, and hub strategy to serve a more premium, globally mobile Indian traveller—positioning Hong Kong not just as a transit point, but as a high-efficiency gateway connecting Hyderabad and other key metros to North America and the wider Asia-Pacific.

title: Adikmet Stepwell Restored, Opened To Public
description: Tourism minister Jupally Krishna Rao inaugurated the stepwell on World Heritage Day in the presence of Vice Chancellor Kumar Molugaram, along with representatives from Dodla Dairy, which supported the restoration.

title: Union Budget 2025-26
description: Budget with significant income tax relief - no tax up to Rs 12 lakh, new simplified regime with 8 slabs. Rs 11.21 lakh crore for infrastructure, support for MSMEs, and Makhana Board for Bihar. Focus on growth, investment, and consumption.

title: One Nation One Election Proposal - 129th Constitutional Amendment Bill
description: Bills introduced in Parliament based on the Ram Nath Kovind committee report to hold simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and State Assemblies. Referred to Joint Parliamentary Committee for detailed examination.

title: National Mission on Natural Farming, 2024
description: Rs 2,481 crore programme to promote chemical-free farming across 1 crore farmers in 15,000 clusters covering all Gram Panchayats. Provides training, bio-input resource centres, and certification for sustainable agricultural practices.

title: PM Internship Scheme, 2024
description: Large-scale internship programme offering 1 crore internship opportunities with top 500 companies over five years. Provides Rs 5,000 monthly allowance and Rs 6,000 one-time assistance targeting youth aged 21-24.

title: PM E-DRIVE Scheme, 2024
description: Rs 10,900 crore scheme replacing FAME-II to promote adoption of electric vehicles. Provides demand incentives for e-2 wheelers, e-3 wheelers, e-ambulances, e-trucks, and supports EV charging infrastructure deployment.

title: Bharatiya Vayuyan Vidheyak (Bharatiya Aircraft Bill), 2024
description: Comprehensive bill to replace the Aircraft Act 1934, modernising India's civil aviation regulatory framework. Addresses drone regulation, air navigation services, and enhanced safety provisions for the growing aviation sector.

title: Disaster Management (Amendment) Act, 2024
description: Amendments to the Disaster Management Act 2005 to establish a comprehensive disaster database, create Urban Disaster Management Authorities, and improve coordination mechanisms between central and state disaster management bodies.

title: Waqf (Amendment) Bill, 2024
description: Proposed amendments to the Waqf Act 1995 to improve governance and transparency of Waqf properties. Introduces digitisation of Waqf records, representation of women and non-Muslims on Waqf boards, and provisions for dispute resolution.

title: Union Budget 2024-25
description: First full budget of NDA III government with focus on employment and skilling (Rs 2 lakh crore for 5 schemes), new income tax regime slabs, abolition of angel tax, and Rs 11.11 lakh crore capex allocation for infrastructure.

title: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
description: Rooftop solar programme providing free electricity up to 300 units per month to 1 crore households through subsidised rooftop solar installations. Government subsidy covers panels up to 3 kW capacity.

title: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA), 2023
description: Three new criminal laws replacing the Indian Penal Code (1860), Code of Criminal Procedure (1898/1973), and Indian Evidence Act (1872). Introduced modern provisions for organised crime, terrorism, and technology-enabled justice delivery.

title: Telecommunications Act, 2023
description: Comprehensive overhaul replacing the Indian Telegraph Act 1885 and Indian Wireless Telegraphy Act 1933. Introduces spectrum assignment by auction or administratively, biometric verification for SIMs, and regulatory sandbox provisions.

title: PM JANMAN - Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan, 2023
description: Rs 24,000 crore scheme for comprehensive development of 75 Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) covering 22,000 villages. Provides housing, safe water, electricity, roads, mobile connectivity, and livelihood support.

title: National Green Credit Programme Rules, 2023
description: Market-based mechanism to incentivise environmental actions through tradeable Green Credits across eight areas including tree plantation, water management, sustainable agriculture, and waste management.

title: Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam (Women's Reservation Act), 2023
description: Reserves one-third of seats in Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies for women, with sub-reservation for SC/ST women. To be implemented after delimitation exercise following the next Census.

title: PM Vishwakarma Yojana, 2023
description: Scheme for traditional artisans and craftspeople working with their hands and tools, covering 18 trades including carpentry, blacksmithing, and pottery. Provides skill training, toolkit incentives, collateral-free credit up to Rs 3 lakh, and digital payment incentives.

title: Mediation Act, 2023
description: India's first standalone mediation law to promote and facilitate mediation for dispute resolution. Establishes Mediation Council of India, provides for community mediation, and gives statutory recognition to mediated settlement agreements.

title: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
description: India's first comprehensive data protection legislation governing the processing of digital personal data. Establishes Data Protection Board, defines data principal and fiduciary rights and obligations, and provides for significant penalties.

title: Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2023
description: Decriminalised 183 offences across 42 central Acts to promote ease of living and doing business. Replaced imprisonment with monetary penalties and compounding for minor regulatory non-compliance offences.

title: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (NRF) Act, 2023
description: Established the National Research Foundation with Rs 50,000 crore over five years to seed, grow, and facilitate research across natural sciences, engineering, technology, humanities, and social sciences in Indian universities and institutions.

title: Offshore Areas Mineral (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023
description: Opens up India's Exclusive Economic Zone for commercial mining of offshore minerals. Establishes a framework for granting licences and leases for offshore mineral exploration and production.

title: Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023
description: Amended the MMDR Act to allow private sector participation in exploration of critical and strategic minerals including lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements. Introduced auction of exploration licences for deep-seated minerals.

title: Central Universities (Amendment) Act, 2023 - Establishment of Tribal University
description: Established the Sammakka Sarakka Central Tribal University in Telangana for the educational empowerment of tribal communities. Named after Gond tribal icons Sammakka and Sarakka.

title: Vibrant Villages Programme
description: Comprehensive development programme for villages along the northern border including those in Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, and Ladakh. Provides infrastructure, livelihood, and tourism development to counter depopulation.

title: National Green Hydrogen Mission, 2023
description: Rs 19,744 crore mission to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Targets 5 million tonnes per annum of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, reducing fossil fuel dependence.

title: National Logistics Policy, 2022
description: Aims to reduce logistics cost from 13-14% of GDP to global best of 8% through efficiency improvements. Establishes Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP), Ease of Logistics (ELOG), and System Improvement Group.

title: National Education Policy Implementation - PM SHRI Schools
description: Development of over 14,500 PM Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI) to showcase NEP 2020 implementation. Provides green, safe, inclusive, and stimulating learning environments with emphasis on modern pedagogy.

title: Electricity (Amendment) Act, 2022 - Proposed Reforms
description: Amendments to strengthen the regulatory framework for power distribution, promote renewable energy, and enable delicensing of distribution for increased competition. Introduces provisions for payment security mechanism.

title: Agnipath Scheme for Armed Forces Recruitment, 2022
description: New short-term military recruitment scheme for soldiers called Agniveers with four-year tenure across Army, Navy, and Air Force. 25% retained permanently after four years; others receive a Seva Nidhi package.

title: Amrit Sarovar Mission
description: Mission to develop and rejuvenate 75 water bodies in each district totalling approximately 50,000 Amrit Sarovars across the country as part of Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav. Focus on community participation and convergence of schemes.

title: New India Literacy Programme (NILP)
description: Successor to Saakshar Bharat, targeting functional literacy and numeracy for 5 crore non-literate adults aged 15+ during 2022-27. Emphasises digital literacy, financial literacy, and critical life skills using online teaching mode.

title: India Semiconductor Mission, 2021
description: Rs 76,000 crore programme to establish a semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem in India. Provides fiscal support of up to 50% of project cost for setting up semiconductor fabs, OSAT, and design centres.

title: Repeal of Farm Laws, 2021
description: Government repealed all three farm laws through the Farm Laws Repeal Act 2021, following over a year of farmer protests. Also announced a committee on MSP and other agricultural reform demands.

title: PM Gati Shakti - National Master Plan for Multi-modal Connectivity
description: Integrated multimodal infrastructure planning platform using GIS-based tools to coordinate projects of 16 ministries. Rs 100 lakh crore plan covering highways, railways, airports, ports, waterways, and logistics infrastructure.

title: National Digital Health Mission (Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission)
description: Creates a digital health ecosystem with unique health IDs, digitised health records, and a registry of health facilities and professionals. Enables interoperable sharing of health data across the healthcare system.

title: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
description: Incentive scheme across 14 key sectors including electronics, pharmaceuticals, automobiles, and textiles with total outlay of Rs 1.97 lakh crore. Aims to boost domestic manufacturing, create jobs, and reduce import dependence.

title: Labour Codes, 2020 - Four Labour Code Acts
description: Consolidated 29 central labour laws into four codes: Wages, Industrial Relations, Social Security, and Occupational Safety. Aimed to simplify compliance, extend social security, and improve ease of doing business.

title: Farm Laws 2020 - Agricultural Reform Bills
description: Three laws enacted to deregulate agricultural markets: Farmers' Produce Trade and Commerce Act, Farmers' Agreement on Price Assurance Act, and Essential Commodities (Amendment) Act. Triggered prolonged farmer protests.

title: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
description: Rs 20,050 crore flagship scheme for sustainable and responsible development of fisheries sector. Aims to enhance fish production by 70 lakh tonnes, generate 55 lakh employment opportunities, and double fisheries exports.

title: National Education Policy (NEP), 2020
description: First comprehensive education policy in 34 years replacing the 1986 policy. Introduces 5+3+3+4 school structure, multidisciplinary higher education, mother tongue instruction till Grade 5, and targets 50% GER in higher education by 2035.

title: PM SVANidhi - PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi
description: Micro-credit scheme providing affordable working capital loans (Rs 10,000 to Rs 50,000 in three tranches) to street vendors affected by COVID-19. Includes digital payment incentives and cashback rewards.

title: Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan (Self-Reliant India) Economic Package
description: Rs 20 lakh crore (approximately 10% of GDP) comprehensive economic stimulus package announced during COVID-19 pandemic. Covered MSMEs, farmers, migrant workers, and structural reforms across agriculture, coal, defence, and space sectors.

title: Citizenship (Amendment) Act (CAA), 2019
description: Amended the Citizenship Act 1955 to provide a path to citizenship for persecuted religious minorities (Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis, Christians) from Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Afghanistan who arrived before December 2014.

title: Jal Jeevan Mission - Har Ghar Jal Expansion
description: Flagship programme to provide functional household tap water connections to every rural household by 2024. From 3.23 crore connections in 2019 to over 14 crore connections, transforming rural water supply infrastructure.

title: Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019 - Article 370 Abrogation
description: Revoked the special autonomous status of Jammu & Kashmir under Article 370, bifurcated the state into two Union Territories: J&K (with legislature) and Ladakh (without legislature). One of the most significant constitutional changes in Indian history.

title: Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act, 2019 - Triple Talaq Ban
description: Criminalised instant triple talaq (talaq-e-biddat) with up to three years imprisonment and fine. Declared the practice void and illegal, providing maintenance and custody rights to affected Muslim women.

title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: Direct income support scheme providing Rs 6,000 per year in three instalments to all landholding farmer families. Over 11 crore farmers covered with total disbursement exceeding Rs 3 lakh crore.

title: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY), 2018
description: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme providing Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalisation. Covers over 55 crore beneficiaries from vulnerable families.

title: Fugitive Economic Offenders Act, 2018
description: Enables confiscation of assets and properties of economic offenders who flee India to avoid criminal prosecution. Applies to offences involving amounts of Rs 100 crore or more.

title: POSHAN Abhiyaan - PM's Overarching Scheme for Holistic Nourishment
description: National nutrition mission targeting reduction of stunting, undernutrition, anaemia, and low birth weight among children, adolescent girls, and pregnant/lactating women. Uses technology-driven real-time monitoring across all districts.

title: Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana (Saubhagya)
description: Universal household electrification scheme to provide electricity connections to all willing un-electrified households in rural and urban areas. Achieved near-universal household electrification by March 2019.

title: Goods and Services Tax (GST) Acts, 2017
description: India's biggest indirect tax reform unifying over a dozen central and state taxes into a single nationwide tax. The 101st Constitutional Amendment and four GST Acts created a unified national market for goods and services.

title: National Health Policy, 2017
description: Comprehensive health policy raising public health expenditure target to 2.5% of GDP. Emphasised Health and Wellness Centres for universal primary healthcare, health assurance, and preventive and promotive healthcare.

title: Demonetisation of Rs 500 and Rs 1000 Notes
description: Overnight withdrawal of 86% of currency in circulation to combat black money, counterfeit currency, and terror financing. Led to rapid growth of digital payments and formalisation of the economy, though debated for its economic impact.

title: UDAN (Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik) - Regional Connectivity Scheme
description: Regional air connectivity scheme to make flying affordable for the common citizen by capping airfares on unserved and underserved routes. Revival of unused airports and subsidised operations for airlines.

title: Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016
description: Unified insolvency resolution framework replacing multiple overlapping laws. Established NCLT as adjudicating authority with time-bound resolution (180+90 days) for corporate insolvency, improving India's ease of doing business.

title: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line households to replace unclean cooking fuels. Over 10 crore connections released, significantly reducing indoor air pollution and health risks for rural women.

title: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Facilitates bank loans between Rs 10 lakh and Rs 1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises. Promotes entrepreneurship among underrepresented groups.

title: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act (RERA), 2016
description: Established Real Estate Regulatory Authorities in each state to protect homebuyers and promote transparency. Mandated project registration, escrow accounts for 70% of funds, and penalties for delays.

title: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
description: Gave statutory backing to Aadhaar as a tool for targeted delivery of subsidies and services. Established UIDAI as a statutory authority with data protection provisions and authentication framework.

title: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: Crop insurance scheme with lowest-ever premium rates (2% for kharif, 1.5% for rabi) providing comprehensive risk cover for pre-sowing to post-harvest losses. Uses technology including satellite imagery and drones for rapid claim settlement.

title: Start-Up India Initiative
description: Action plan to promote entrepreneurship with tax exemptions for three years, self-certification for compliance, simplified startup registration, and a Rs 10,000 crore Fund of Funds. Recognised over 1 lakh startups.

title: BharatNet - National Optical Fibre Network
description: Project to connect all 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats with broadband through optical fibre, providing 100 Mbps connectivity. Critical digital infrastructure backbone for rural India's internet connectivity.

title: Skill India Mission - Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
description: Flagship skill development scheme to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training with monetary rewards and certification. Target of skilling one crore youth over four years.

title: Digital India Programme
description: Comprehensive initiative to transform India into a digitally empowered society with three pillars: digital infrastructure, digital governance, and digital literacy. Umbrella programme covering e-governance, broadband, and digital services.

title: Smart Cities Mission
description: Urban renewal programme to develop 100 smart cities with core infrastructure, sustainable environment, and technology-driven solutions. Total investment of Rs 2.05 lakh crore across selected cities.

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Housing for All
description: Comprehensive housing scheme targeting construction of 2 crore houses in urban areas and 2.95 crore in rural areas by 2024. Provides interest subsidy on home loans and direct assistance for house construction.

title: Sagarmala Programme
description: Port-led development programme to modernise India's ports, enhance port connectivity, develop coastal economic zones, and promote coastal shipping. Envisaged investment of over Rs 8 lakh crore.

title: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
description: Provides micro-finance loans up to Rs 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises through three categories: Shishu (up to Rs 50,000), Kishore (up to Rs 5 lakh), and Tarun (up to Rs 10 lakh).

title: Insurance Laws (Amendment) Act, 2015
description: Raised FDI cap in insurance sector from 26% to 49%, strengthened IRDA's regulatory powers, and allowed Lloyd's of London to operate in India. Aimed to increase insurance penetration and capital inflows.

title: Swachh Bharat Mission (Clean India Mission)
description: Nationwide campaign to eliminate open defecation and improve solid waste management. Built over 11 crore toilets in rural and urban areas and declared India open-defecation free by October 2019.

title: Make in India Initiative
description: Flagship initiative to transform India into a global manufacturing hub across 25 sectors. Simplified regulatory environment, dedicated investor facilitation, and robust intellectual property regime to boost domestic and foreign investment.

title: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
description: World's largest financial inclusion programme providing universal access to banking services with zero-balance accounts, RuPay debit cards, and insurance cover. Over 50 crore accounts opened since launch.

title: Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013
description: Established the Lokpal (federal anti-corruption ombudsman) and required states to appoint Lokayuktas within one year. Covers the Prime Minister, ministers, and MPs with jurisdiction over corruption complaints.

title: Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement (LARR) Act, 2013
description: Replaced the colonial Land Acquisition Act of 1894 with provisions for fair compensation (2-4x market value), mandatory consent of landowners, social impact assessment, and rehabilitation of displaced families.

title: National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013
description: Provides subsidised foodgrains to approximately 81 crore people (two-thirds of the population) through the Targeted Public Distribution System. Guarantees 5 kg per person per month at Rs 1-3/kg for rice, wheat, and coarse grains.

title: Companies Act, 2013
description: Comprehensive overhaul of corporate governance law replacing the Companies Act 1956. Introduced mandatory CSR spending (2% of net profit), class action suits, one-person companies, and enhanced auditor accountability.

title: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013
description: Defines and prohibits sexual harassment at the workplace and establishes Internal Complaints Committees and Local Complaints Committees for redressal. Applies to both organised and unorganised sectors.

title: Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2013
description: Comprehensive reform of criminal law on sexual offences enacted after the 2012 Delhi gang rape. Widened the definition of rape, introduced new offences like stalking, voyeurism, and acid attack, and enhanced penalties.

title: Rajiv Gandhi Panchayat Sashaktikaran Abhiyan (RGPSA)
description: Centrally sponsored scheme to strengthen Panchayati Raj institutions by building capacities of elected representatives and functionaries. Aimed to improve local governance, accountability, and devolution of powers to gram panchayats.

title: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) Launched
description: Pioneered electronic transfer of government subsidies and benefits directly to beneficiary bank accounts using Aadhaar authentication. Aimed at reducing leakages, eliminating middlemen, and improving targeting of welfare schemes.

title: 12th Five Year Plan (2012-2017): Faster, More Inclusive and Sustainable Growth
description: India's last Five Year Plan targeting 8% GDP growth with emphasis on health, education, and sustainable development. Introduced focus on governance improvements and the shift towards outcome-based monitoring.

title: FDI in Multi-Brand Retail - Policy Liberalisation
description: Allowed 51% FDI in multi-brand retail and 100% in single-brand retail, subject to conditions including 30% local sourcing. Aimed to attract global retail chains and modernise India's retail infrastructure.

title: National Manufacturing Policy, 2011
description: Aimed to increase manufacturing sector's share of GDP from 16% to 25% by 2022 and create 100 million additional jobs. Proposed National Investment and Manufacturing Zones (NIMZs) with simplified regulations.

title: National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM) - Aajeevika
description: Restructured poverty alleviation programme replacing SGSY to reduce rural poverty through self-managed Self Help Groups (SHGs). Mobilised over 9 crore women into 83 lakh SHGs with bank linkages for sustainable livelihoods.

title: Rajiv Awas Yojana (RAY) for Slum-Free India
description: Scheme to make India slum-free by encouraging states to assign property rights to slum dwellers, upgrade existing slums, and provide affordable housing for the urban poor.

title: Aadhaar First Enrolment and Rollout
description: First Aadhaar number issued to a resident of Tembhli village, Maharashtra on 29 September 2010. Marked the operational launch of the world's largest biometric identity programme, crossing 100 crore enrolments by 2016.

title: National Food Security Mission (NFSM) Expansion
description: Expanded food security mission to cover 482 districts in rice, wheat, and pulses production. Targeted additional production of 25 million tonnes of foodgrains through improved technology dissemination and farm-level demonstrations.

title: Jawaharlal Nehru National Solar Mission (JNNSM) Phase I
description: Part of NAPCC, targeting 20 GW of solar capacity by 2022 (later revised to 100 GW). Introduced feed-in tariffs, viability gap funding, and RPO mechanisms to promote grid-connected solar power.

title: Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education (RTE) Act, 2009
description: Made free and compulsory education a fundamental right for children aged 6-14 years under Article 21A. Mandated 25% reservation in private schools for economically weaker sections and set pupil-teacher ratios.

title: Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA)
description: Centrally sponsored scheme to universalise access to and improve quality of secondary education (Classes IX-X). Target to achieve 75% enrolment ratio by providing infrastructure, teacher training, and removing gender and socio-economic barriers.

title: National Skill Development Policy and NSDC Expansion
description: Expanded the National Skill Development Corporation's mandate to skill 150 million people by 2022 through public-private partnerships. Set up Sector Skill Councils and standardised vocational training frameworks.

title: Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) and Aadhaar Launch
description: Established UIDAI to issue 12-digit unique identification numbers (Aadhaar) to all Indian residents. First Aadhaar number issued in September 2010. Became the world's largest biometric identification system.

title: Unorganised Workers' Social Security Act, 2008
description: Provided social security framework for unorganised sector workers covering life and disability cover, health and maternity benefits, and old age protection. Established National and State Social Security Boards.

title: Indo-US Civil Nuclear Agreement
description: Landmark agreement enabling civil nuclear cooperation between India and the United States, ending India's nuclear isolation. Allowed India access to civilian nuclear technology and fuel from the global market.

title: National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), 2008
description: India's first comprehensive climate policy framework comprising eight national missions including Solar, Energy Efficiency, Water, Himalayan Ecosystem, Green India, Sustainable Agriculture, Sustainable Habitat, and Strategic Knowledge for Climate Change.

title: Agricultural Debt Waiver and Debt Relief Scheme, 2008
description: Waived loans of approximately 3.6 crore small and marginal farmers at a cost of Rs 71,680 crore. One of the largest farm debt waiver programmes in Indian history, covering both direct and indirect agricultural loans.

title: 11th Five Year Plan (2007-2012): Towards Faster and More Inclusive Growth
description: Planning Commission blueprint targeting 9% GDP growth with focus on inclusive development, education, health, and rural infrastructure. Emphasised bridging divides between rural-urban and rich-poor populations.

title: National Rehabilitation and Resettlement Policy, 2007
description: Policy framework to address displacement caused by development projects, providing rehabilitation packages to affected families. Aimed to minimise displacement and ensure resettlement with dignity.

title: Semiconductor Policy, 2007
description: India's early policy framework to attract semiconductor fabrication investments with incentives of up to 25% of capital expenditure. Aimed to position India in the global electronics manufacturing ecosystem.

title: Central Educational Institutions (Reservation in Admission) Act, 2006 - OBC Reservations
description: Mandated 27% reservation for Other Backward Classes in central educational institutions including IITs, IIMs, and central universities. Implemented after Supreme Court upheld its constitutional validity.

title: Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006
description: Recognises the rights of forest-dwelling tribal communities and other traditional forest dwellers to forest land and resources. Corrects historical injustice by vesting land and resource rights.

title: Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojana (PMSSY)
description: Scheme to set up AIIMS-like institutions across India and upgrade existing government medical college institutions. Aimed at correcting regional imbalances in availability of affordable tertiary healthcare.

title: Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission (JNNURM), 2005
description: Massive urban infrastructure programme targeting 63 cities for integrated development of urban infrastructure, services, and governance reforms. India's first large-scale urban rejuvenation initiative.

title: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
description: Provides comprehensive civil law protection to women from domestic violence, covering physical, sexual, verbal, emotional, and economic abuse. Introduced protection officers and shelter homes.

title: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), 2005
description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work. The world's largest work guarantee programme, covering over 700 districts.

title: Special Economic Zones (SEZ) Act, 2005
description: Established a framework for creation of Special Economic Zones to promote exports, attract FDI, and generate employment. Provided tax holidays and single-window clearance for SEZ developers and units.

title: Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005
description: Landmark transparency legislation empowering citizens to request information from public authorities, replacing the Freedom of Information Act 2002. Widely regarded as one of the strongest accountability tools in Indian democracy.

title: National Rural Health Mission (NRHM), 2005
description: Comprehensive healthcare initiative to provide accessible, affordable and quality healthcare to rural populations, especially vulnerable groups. Introduced ASHA workers at the village level across India.

title: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA) Expansion
description: Major expansion of the universal elementary education programme aimed at providing quality education to all children aged 6-14 years. Enhanced funding, teacher recruitment, and infrastructure development across states.

title: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) Phase II
description: Expansion of the rural roads programme to provide all-weather connectivity to unconnected habitations with population of 500+ in plain areas and 250+ in hilly and tribal areas.
